title: Reality Check Chinas Appetite For A New Stockpiling Wave
section_path: Reality Check Chinas Appetite For A New Stockpiling Wave
section_path_text: Reality Check Chinas Appetite For A New Stockpiling Wave
summary:
Main
China's onshore crude inventories reached all-time highs in August, raising the question of whether another wave of stockpiling is on the horizon. By Emma Li
So far this year, China's seaborne crude imports have averaged 10.3 mbd-about 3% higher than the same period in 2024. But if we strip out stock builds, adjusted imports have been consistently lower year-on-year from March to August.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
China's onshore crude inventories reached all-time highs in August, raising the question of whether another wave of stockpiling is on the horizon. By Emma Li
So far this year, China's seaborne crude imports have averaged 10.3 mbd-about 3% higher than the same period in 2024.

children.title: State-owned builds stalled, capacity expansion on the horizon
section_path: State-owned builds stalled, capacity expansion on the horizon
children.section_path_text: State-owned builds stalled, capacity expansion on the horizon
children.summary: In Q2, state-owned refiners (SOEs) built close to 60mb of crude into storage, almost all in commercial tanks, lifting above-ground inventories to record highs of more than 730mb. SOEs are unique in that they face no import quota restrictions and are tasked with meeting Beijing's stockpile mandates.

children.title: Shandong inventories rise on robust Iranian inflows
section_path: Shandong inventories rise on robust Iranian inflows
children.section_path_text: Shandong inventories rise on robust Iranian inflows
children.summary: In Shandong, home to the independent "teapot" refiners, commercial stocks have continued to climb over the past six months, even as SOE builds paused. This has been driven by a strong inflow of sanctioned crude-especially Iranian barrels-as traders and refiners seek to move volumes onshore to mitigate risks from potential Western sanctions on tankers or port facilities.

children.title: Outlook
children.summary: Taken together, China appears unlikely to sustain another strong wave of crude stockpiling in the near term. SOE inventories are already at record highs with limited spare capacity until new tanks come online from 2026 onward.
